rationale: Keep: the cited paper is directly about the attraction effect (a focal construct in the citing paper) and offers a specific mechanism (trade-off aversion/negative emotion) that could serve as theoretical grounding rather than a generic mention. The citing paper explicitly models attraction alongside other context effects and positions itself as extending prior context-effect literature, making this 2009 JMR attraction paper plausibly substantive background. With only DOI-level match evidence and no in-text snippet, it is not safe to treat this as obvious mention-only.
